name: rapp-cave
tagline: The public workshop. Park a cubby, share rapplications and eggs, pull anyone's work — same cubby mechanism, fully open.
purpose: The public batcave: a standalone public workspace-neighborhood (the public-workspace pattern). Anyone can park a cubby — isolated housing for their FULL rapp estate (agents, organs, senses, rapplications, whole neighborhoods, eggs) — the same environment as their on-device brainstem. Anyone can stream agents from any cubby into their local brainstem without grail-repo commit risk, share back via fork+PR, and read what's new on the show-and-tell stream. Identical cubby mechanics to the private batcave, with the gate removed: browse, pull, and join are open to the world.

quirks.isolation: owner-only writes per cubby (one cubby per GitHub login); append-only shared zones; cross-cubby contribution via PR
quirks.streaming: cave load → target brainstem agents/ + .git/info/exclude (zero commit risk); pull is a plain curl/clone, no auth required
quirks.merge_rule: (from, ts) is the universal key; append-only union

membership_policy.notes: PUBLIC join path. No out-of-band gate, no operator approval required. Anyone forks https://github.com/kody-w/RAPP, adds their cubby under cave/cubbies/<github-login>/, and opens a PR — or simply pulls others' cubbies with plain curl/clone. Write access to your own cubby is granted by the PR merge; the world has read access by default.

branch_model.main: shared truth — cubby-scoped writes + append-only zones, landed via PR
branch_model.personal: cubby/<handle>/<topic> on your fork — yours; never required to merge

addresses._note: A public front door by design. Anyone can browse the Pages site, pull any cubby over plain curl, and join by fork + PR. No auth, no collaborator gate, no dial tone — the cave is open.
